topics.text: Ancient India — Indus Valley, Vedic, Mauryan, Gupta, post-Gupta periods
Medieval India — Delhi Sultanate, Vijayanagara, Mughals, Bhakti & Sufi movements
Modern India — 1757 to 1947; East India Company to Independence
Indian National Movement — 1857 revolt, Moderates & Extremists, Gandhi era, INA
Art & Architecture — temple styles, sculptures, paintings (Ajanta, Mughal, etc.)
Literature, music, dance, performing arts
Fairs, festivals, folk traditions
Physical Geography — geomorphology, climatology, oceanography
Indian Geography — physiographic divisions, rivers, soils, natural vegetation
Economic Geography — agriculture, industry, resources
World Geography — continents, major features, climate zones
Maps and atlases — location of places in news
Constitution — Preamble, Schedules, Amendments
Fundamental Rights, DPSPs, Fundamental Duties
Parliament, Executive, Judiciary
Constitutional bodies — EC, CAG, UPSC, Finance Commission
Local governance — Panchayati Raj, 73rd/74th Amendments
Welfare schemes and policies
Basic economic concepts — GDP, GNP, inflation, fiscal/monetary policy
Indian economy — agriculture, industry, services, trade
Budget, taxation, RBI, banking
Government schemes — PM Awas, MNREGA, Jan Dhan, etc.
International organisations — IMF, World Bank, WTO
Ecosystems, biodiversity, food chains, national parks & wildlife sanctuaries
Climate change — greenhouse gases, IPCC, Paris Agreement
Environmental laws and conventions — CITES, Ramsar, CBD
Pollution — air, water, soil; NGT
Environmental Impact Assessment; CAMPA; Green India Mission
Physics basics — motion, electricity, light, sound
Chemistry — elements, compounds, everyday chemistry
Biology — cell, genetics, diseases, nutrition, human body systems
Space — ISRO missions, satellites, recent launches
Defence & nuclear technology; AI, robotics, biotech in news
Reading comprehension — passage-based questions
English language comprehension skills
Series, analogies, odd-one-out, coding-decoding
Blood relations, direction sense, seating arrangement
Syllogisms, statements & assumptions, inferences
Critical reasoning, argument analysis
Number system, HCF/LCM, simplification
Percentage, profit-loss, ratio-proportion
Time-speed-distance, time & work
Averages, mixtures, mensuration
Data interpretation — tables, bar charts, pie charts, line graphs
Decision-making and problem-solving scenarios
Interpersonal skills, general mental ability

gs1.title: Mains — GS Paper I (History, Geography, Society)

topics.text: Salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times
Modern Indian history from 1750 — significant events, personalities, issues
Freedom Struggle — its various stages and important contributors
Post-independence consolidation & reorganisation within the country
History of the world — colonisation, de-colonisation, world wars, cold war
Salient features of Indian Society, diversity of India
Role of women and women's organisations; Social empowerment
Communalism, Regionalism, Secularism
Urbanisation — growth and associated problems
Population and associated issues; Poverty and developmental issues; Globalisation and effects on Indian society
Physical Geography — Salient features of world's physical geography
Distribution of key natural resources across the world
Changes in critical geographical features and flora & fauna
Important geophysical phenomena — earthquakes, tsunamis, volcanoes, cyclones

gs2.title: Mains — GS Paper II (Polity, Governance, IR)

topics.text: Indian Constitution — historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions
Functions and responsibilities of Union and States; Centre-State relations
Parliament and State Legislatures — structure, functioning, conduct of business
Executive and Judiciary — structure, organisation, functioning
Ministries and Departments of Government; Pressure Groups and formal/informal associations
Appointment to various Constitutional positions; powers, functions and responsibilities
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors
Development processes and the development industry; NGOs, SHGs, etc.
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections; mechanisms, laws, institutions for protection
Issues relating to health, education, human resources
Issues related to poverty and hunger; Important aspects of governance — transparency, accountability, e-Governance
India's foreign policy; Indian diaspora
India and its neighbourhood — bilateral relations
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India's interests
Important international institutions, agencies and fora — their structure, mandate
Bilateral, regional and global groupings — SAARC, BRICS, G20, SCO, etc.

gs3.title: Mains — GS Paper III (Economy, Environment, Security)

topics.text: Indian economy and planning — inclusive growth, budgeting, mobilisation of resources
Agriculture — irrigation, farm subsidies, MSP, food security, PDS
Infrastructure — energy, ports, roads, airports; investment models
Science and Technology — applications in agriculture, food processing
Industrial policy and growth; effects of globalisation
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; EIA
Disaster and disaster management — NDMA, vulnerable areas
Linkages between development and spread of extremism; role of external state and non-state actors
Developments and their applications in everyday life; achievements of Indians in S&T
Indigenisation of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology
Intellectual property rights
Challenges to internal security — terrorism, insurgency, left-wing extremism
Security forces and agencies; cybersecurity
Money-laundering and its prevention
Border management; organised crime and terrorism linkages

gs4.title: Mains — GS Paper IV (Ethics, Integrity & Aptitude)

topics.text: Essence, determinants and consequences of Ethics in human actions
Dimensions of Ethics; Ethics in private and public relationships
Human Values — lessons from lives and teachings of great leaders, reformers and administrators
Role of family, society and educational institutions in inculcating values
Attitude — content, structure, function; its influence on thought and behaviour
Aptitude and foundational values for Civil Services — integrity, impartiality, non-partisanship, dedication to public service
Emotional intelligence — concepts, utilities and application in administration and governance
Contributions of moral thinkers and philosophers from India and the world
Public/civil service values and ethics in Public administration
Status and problems; ethical concerns in government and private institutions
Probity in Governance — concept of public service; philosophical basis of governance and probity
Information sharing and transparency in government; RTI; codes of ethics, codes of conduct
Citizen's Charters; work culture; quality of service delivery; utilisation of public funds
Case Studies — applying ethical principles to real situations
Meaning, scope and development of Anthropology
Relationships with Social Sciences, Biological Sciences, Medical Sciences, Earth Sciences, Humanities

Human Evolution and emergence of Man — biological and cultural factors
Family — definition, types, functions, changing perspectives
Marriage — definition, types, functions; marriage regulations; marriage payments
Kinship — terminology, descent, lineage, clan, phratry, moiety
Residence rules; preferential and prescriptive marriage

Formalist vs Substantivist debate; Modes of exchange — reciprocity, redistribution, market
Concepts of private property, land tenure, concepts of money
Hunters & gatherers, pastoralism, horticulture, agriculture, industrialism
Band, tribe, chiefdom, kingdom, state — political typologies
Concept of power, authority, legitimacy
Social control, law and justice in simple societies

Animism, animatism, totemism, taboo, fetishism
Shamanism; sects and cults; myth and ritual
Functions of magic, sorcery and witchcraft

Historical Particularism — Boas; Diffusionism — British & German
Functionalism — Malinowski; Structural-Functionalism — Radcliffe-Brown
Structuralism — Lévi-Strauss; Culture & Personality school
Neo-evolutionism — White, Steward; Cultural ecology
Marxist anthropology; Symbolic & Interpretive anthropology
Post-modern anthropology; feminist anthropology

Language and culture; language and cognition; non-verbal communication
Social context of language use; sociolinguistics
Fieldwork tradition in anthropology; participant observation
Interview methods, genealogical method, case study, extended case method
Analysis of quantitative and qualitative data; sampling methods
Ethics of anthropological research
Methods for study of genetic principles in man

ABO, Rh blood groups; HLA; chromosomal aberrations

Race and intelligence debate; racism; racial hybridity

Concept of biological age; determination of sex

Contributions of tribal cultures to Indian civilisation

Ethno-archaeological research in India — case studies
Ethnic and linguistic elements in Indian population and their distribution

Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes — size and distribution
Varnashrama; purushartha; karma, rina and punarjanma; concepts of pollution and purity
Jajmani system; sacred complex; nature-man-spirit complex
Caste system — origin, characteristics, untouchability, changes; inter-caste relations
Historical development of Indian anthropology — colonial to contemporary
Contributions of Indian anthropologists — G.S. Ghurye, M.N. Srinivas, Verrier Elwin, S.C. Dube
Unity in diversity — integration, assimilation; 'little' and 'great' tradition

Nutritional status, health problems, reproductive health
Alcoholism and substance abuse; problems of displacement and rehabilitation

Land alienation, forest rights; FRA 2006; tribal land reform
ITDP, TSP (Tribal Sub-Plan), MADA, Clusters; role of NGOs

Tribal and agrarian movements; socio-religious reform movements
Impact of development projects on tribal societies; globalisation impact
